description: Willy has a thought, releases the thought, watches the thought float away, and repeats the process.
Lizzy wallows in sadness after being rejected by his long-time school crush, Sally. Mrs. Lizardino is unsympathetic to her self-pitying son, and lets him know it.
Froggy gets anxious when he is paired with Chimpzo—a classmate with a bad reputation. Ms. Mouse reprimands Froggy, with the goal of promoting inclusivity in her classroom.
Bucky, who is introverted by nature, has always dreaded drama class. His mandatory participation in the class's latest game leaves him feeling exposed and anxious—as he expected it would.
Donkey prefers to avoid all conflict in life. This strategy, while manageable for the moment, will almost assuredly be proven unsustainable in the long-run.
Chamie's mother catches him watching violent videos on his tablet and puts a stop to it. He promptly discovers some age-appropriate content.

Billy is caught reading comics in class by Mr. Roboto, who seizes the opportunity to deliver a lesson on the benefits of delayed gratification. Billy listens carefully, but misinterprets his teacher's lesson. Mr. Goatwan is not pleased.

Old Man Barry is enamoured with the shelving inside the neighbourhood's newest hat store. Store employee Spotty Salamando can only humour Barry for so long before erupting into a rather unprofessional fit of rage.
Squidzi and Monk attend a baseball game together. Squidzi is absorbed by the statistics. Monk is absorbed by the baseball. Both have a great time.
Willy and Froggy enjoy a golf outing on a sunny autumn day. Willy is unimpressed by Froggy's drive and says so. This irritates Froggy, who stands up for himself.

Mr. and Mrs. Bear argue over which route they should take to their destination. Mr. Bear—who is behind the wheel of the automobile—ultimately decides. Unforeseen events leave Mrs. Bear feeling vindicated.
Joey is a firm believer in unwritten social conventions, and is troubled to find that his classmates are not.  His frustrations culminate in his berating of Sharkido in the cafeteria.
Chamie wants to play with Leon's new—and very delicate—model spaceship. Leon has to come up with a plan to appease Chamie while keeping his prized possession safe.

Froggy and Alphie are playing a popular trading card game during a geography class work period. They are interrupted by Mr. Raven, who is astonished—and somewhat annoyed—by Froggy's gift for subject-specific recollection.

Paca wants to know Mrs. Applebee's age. Mrs. Applebee decides to turn Paca's unintentionally rude question into a guessing game. Paca tries his best to come up with the correct answer, but eventually gives up.

Fishree is finally old enough to buy her first mobile phone. She hasn't done much research, but has a vague idea of which model would best suit her needs. Hedgey tries his best to be a helpful salesperson.
Willy asks Froggy if he is going to the jamboree. Froggy is planning to go, and is especially excited for the fireworks. Willy is frustrated by Froggy's misuse of a common English expression.
Mrs. Applebee attempts to persuade Bluey into sweeping sparkles off the floor, but her plan is ultimately thwarted by Bluey's playful spirit. She tries not to get too frustrated.

Snowy's father bestows upon him generations-old wisdom about courtship. Unfortunately, it's not the advice Snowy had hoped for, leaving him more discouraged than ever in his quest to win Sammy's heart.
Mr. Raven faces a dilemma when Sammy earns a star that he cannot award. Luckily, Billy's classroom antics offer Mr. Raven a clever solution to his predicament.
Mr. Barker scolds Froggy for his lackadaisical warmup. Joey takes notice and launches a hurtful taunt, accidentally catching Toadmo in the crossfire.
